## [06:06 - 08:54]

$60,000 you know back here if he did 200 what would he do like $5,000 or something. So big big difference. Now if you think about it, he was able to sell 200 of these in just a couple weeks during the launch. Normally he's selling $50,000 a month. He was able to sell 10 times as many and he was able to sell them at an 11 times higher price even though it was the same basic information. So that shows you how information is fungible but also when you raise your prices and raise both the perceived value and the real value, it's just a lot easier to make a lot of money. Now another thing is he didn't really have any affiliates back here because let's just say you're paying a 50% commission to affiliates. So a 50% commission would be $13.50. That's in other words if you had a joint venture partner or an affiliate and they sold one of your ebooks and you paid them half they'd make $13.50. Now if you had an affiliate back here it'd be almost $150. It'd be like whatever $148.50. So it's a lot easier to find JV partners and affiliates at $150 per sale than it is at $13.50 per sale. So in addition to it's easier to make a lot of money and it's easier to get people excited about like a home study course and sell more units, it's also easier to attract affiliates who are going to drive more traffic and help you make more sales.
